Entry Age Range: 30 days (completed) to 55/50/45/40 years depending on PPT 15/20/25/30
Premium Payment Term (PPT) & Modes: Regular premium with PPT 15, 20, 25 or 30 years; standard LIC modes (yearly/half-yearly/quarterly/monthly via NACH/salary deduction)
Maximum Sum Assured: No fixed upper limit (subject to underwriting)
Death Benefit: If death occurs before commencement of risk: refund of premiums paid (excluding taxes/extra/rider premiums) without interest. After commencement of risk: Sum Assured on Death (higher of 7 × annualised premium or Basic Sum Assured) plus vested simple reversionary bonuses and Final Additional Bonus; total death benefit not less than 105% of total premiums paid.
Survival Benefit: Annual survival benefit equal to 8% of Basic Sum Assured from the end of the premium paying term each year, so long as the life assured survives, up to the policy anniversary prior to age 100 or earlier death, while policy is in force.
Maturity Benefit: On survival to age 100 (policy term end), Sum Assured on Maturity equal to Basic Sum Assured plus vested simple reversionary bonuses and Final Additional Bonus, if any, is payable as lump sum.
Bonus / Guaranteed Additions: Participating plan with simple reversionary bonuses accruing as declared and a possible Final Additional Bonus at death or maturity; no guaranteed additions.
Surrender Value: Policy can be surrendered after completion of first policy year with one full year’s premium paid; Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) arises after at least two full years’ premiums. On surrender, LIC pays higher of GSV and Special Surrender Value. GSV = total premiums paid × GSV factor plus surrender value of vested bonuses (bonuses × bonus GSV factor); factors depend on policy year as per annexures.
Paid-up Rules: After at least one full year’s premium, if subsequent premiums are not paid, policy becomes paid-up. Death Paid-up Sum Assured and Maturity Paid-up Sum Assured are proportional to the ratio of premiums paid to premiums payable. Paid-up death benefit = Death Paid-up Sum Assured + vested bonuses; paid-up maturity benefit similar. Survival benefit at 8% each year is paid only if Maturity Paid-up Sum Assured ≥ ₹2,00,000; paid-up policies have limited or no participation in future profits as per rules.
Revival Rules: Lapsed policy can be revived within 5 consecutive years from the date of first unpaid premium by paying all arrears of premium with interest at a rate linked to 10-year G-Sec yield or Non-Linked Participating Fund yield (as specified from time to time) and subject to underwriting; revival effective only after LIC’s approval and issuing revival receipt.
Loan Options: Loan available after completion of first policy year with at least one full premium paid and within surrender value. During PPT: for in-force policies, max 50% of surrender value before two full years’ premiums and 75% after; for paid-up policies, 40%/65%. After PPT, maximum loan fixed so that annual loan interest does not exceed 50% of annual survival benefit; interest is compounded half-yearly at LIC-declared rate (capped at 10-year G-Sec + 3% or participating fund yield + 1%). Loan interest can be recovered from survival benefits/other payouts; policy may be foreclosed if loan plus interest would exceed surrender value.
Grace Period: Standard LIC grace period applies (30 days for yearly/half-yearly/quarterly, 15 days for monthly) during which cover continues; if premium not paid by end of grace, policy lapses.
Waiting Period: No separate waiting period for death/survival benefits; only commencement-of-risk rule for minors (risk starts shortly before completion of 2 years from commencement or before policy anniversary after age 8, whichever earlier; for age 8+ at entry, risk starts immediately).
Exclusions: Suicide exclusion: if life assured commits suicide within 12 months of commencement of risk, benefit is 80% of total premiums paid (excluding taxes/extra/rider premiums), provided policy is in force. If suicide occurs within 12 months of revival, higher of 80% of total premiums paid or surrender value is payable; in such cases no other benefits are payable, with specified exceptions for minors and lapsed policies without paid-up value.
Tax Benefits: Statutory taxes (e.g. GST) are extra and not counted for benefit calculations. Possible income-tax benefits on premiums/benefits are as per prevailing Income Tax laws; brochure advises consulting a tax advisor.
Available Riders: LIC’s Accidental Death and Disability Benefit Rider; LIC’s Accident Benefit Rider; LIC’s New Term Assurance Rider; LIC’s Premium Waiver Benefit Rider (subject to eligibility and conditions).
Key Advantages: Whole-life cover up to age 100; guaranteed yearly income of 8% of Basic Sum Assured after PPT; lump-sum benefit at age 100; participation in profits via bonuses; loan facility for liquidity; high Sum Assured rebate; option to receive death benefit in instalments.
Limitations or Gotchas: Very long policy term (to age 100) and limited premium-paying terms mean long commitment; survival income starts only after PPT is over; surrender values build gradually and depend on GSV/SSV factors; bonuses are not guaranteed and can vary; no guaranteed additions; minimum Sum Assured of ₹2 lakh may be high for some customers.

Policy Type: Non-par, non-linked, whole-life savings with guaranteed income (Regular / Flexi Income), no bonuses
Entry Age Range: Entry age and PPT linked: for PPT 5–8 years, min entry 8 years; PPT 9–10: min 7/6 years; PPT 11–15: min 5/4/3/2/1 years; PPT 16: min 30 days; maximum entry age 65 years (nearer birthday) for PPT 5–10, 64/63/62/61/60/59 years for PPT 11–16 respectively.
Maximum Maturity Age: Whole-life cover (no defined maturity age); maximum premium-ceasing age 75 years (nearer birthday).
Policy Term: Whole life (policy continues for lifetime unless surrendered or terminated); income benefits start from a policy year that depends on PPT (11th to 19th policy year).
Premium Payment Term (PPT) & Modes: PPT selectable from 5 to 16 years. Premiums payable in regular modes: yearly, half-yearly, quarterly, monthly (NACH only) or salary deduction, as per LIC norms.
Maximum Sum Assured: No fixed upper limit (subject to underwriting and board-approved policy)
Death Benefit: On death after commencement of risk while policy in force: Death Benefit = Sum Assured on Death + accrued Guaranteed Additions, subject to minimum 105% of total premiums paid. Sum Assured on Death is higher of Basic Sum Assured or 7 × annualised premium. (If death before risk commencement for minors, refund-of-premium rules apply as per standard LIC provisions.)
Survival Benefit: Two options chosen at inception: Option I – Regular Income Benefit, Option II – Flexi Income Benefit. Under Regular Income, on survival from the due start year (based on PPT), the life assured receives a fixed percentage of Basic Sum Assured (typically 10% p.a. for standard cases) every year for life while policy is in force. Under Flexi Income, the same amount is credited yearly as Flexi Income Benefit; policyholder may withdraw all or part anytime, with interest credited on unwithdrawn amounts; paid-up policies have scaled income % based on Paid-up Sum Assured.
Maturity Benefit: No maturity benefit is payable under this plan; benefits are in the form of lifetime income and death benefit plus accrued Guaranteed Additions.
Bonus / Guaranteed Additions: Non-participating plan (no bonuses). Instead, Guaranteed Additions accrue at ₹40 per ₹1,000 of Basic Sum Assured at the end of each policy year during the PPT only. No further GA accrue after PPT; on death or surrender during PPT, GA for that year accrue in full or proportionately, as specified.
Surrender Value: Policy may be surrendered after completion of first policy year with at least one full premium paid. Guaranteed Surrender Value arises after payment of at least two full years’ premiums. On surrender of in-force or paid-up policy, LIC pays higher of Guaranteed and Special Surrender Values. GSV = [total premiums paid × GSV factor] + [accrued Guaranteed Additions × GA GSV factor], reduced by any Regular/Flexi Income Benefits already paid; for Flexi, accumulated unwithdrawn Flexi benefits with interest are also payable. Detailed GSV factors are given in policy annexures/brochure.
Paid-up Rules: On non-payment of premiums after the policy has acquired surrender value, policy becomes paid-up. Death benefit becomes Paid-up Sum Assured plus accrued Guaranteed Additions (subject to minimum 105% of premiums). For Regular/Flexi Income, benefits under a paid-up policy are calculated as a percentage of Paid-up Sum Assured (with slabs for Flexi Income) and continue from the scheduled start year; any unwithdrawn Flexi benefits with interest are payable on death or surrender.
Revival Rules: Lapsed policy can be revived within 5 consecutive years from the date of first unpaid premium by payment of all arrears with interest at a rate linked to 10-year G-Sec or the Non-Linked Non-Participating Fund yield (as specified) and subject to underwriting. Revival becomes effective only after approval and issue of revival receipt; riders (if any) can be revived only along with base policy.
Loan Options: Loan can be availed after completion of first policy year with at least one full premium paid. Before income start year, maximum loan is generally up to 75% of surrender value for in-force and 50% for paid-up policies. From the income start year onwards, loan is restricted so that annual loan interest does not exceed 50% of the annual Regular/Flexi Income Benefit, subject to same 75%/50% caps on surrender value; under Flexi option, additional loan up to 50% of Flexi Income Benefits due and not withdrawn is allowed. Interest is compounded half-yearly at a rate capped at 10-year G-Sec + 3% or Non-Linked Non-Participating fund yield +1%; loan interest can be recovered from Regular/Flexi income, and policies may be foreclosed if loan plus interest would exceed surrender value (plus accumulated Flexi income, for Option II).
Grace Period: Grace period of 30 days for yearly/half-yearly/quarterly premiums and 15 days for monthly premiums from date of first unpaid premium; policy remains in force during grace with full risk cover; if premium not paid by end of grace, policy lapses.
Waiting Period: No separate waiting period; risk commencement for minors follows standard rule: if age at entry <8, risk starts 2 years from commencement or from policy anniversary after age 8 (whichever earlier); for age 8+ at entry, risk commences immediately.
Exclusions: Standard suicide exclusion: if suicide within 12 months of commencement of risk, benefit is 80% of total premiums paid (excluding taxes/extra/rider premiums), provided policy in force. If suicide within 12 months of revival, higher of 80% of premiums paid or surrender value is payable; clause not applicable for minors at entry below 8 years or lapsed policies without paid-up value, as specified.
Tax Benefits: Statutory taxes (including GST) are extra on premiums (including rider and extra premiums) and are not counted for benefit calculations. Any income-tax benefits on premiums/benefits are as per prevailing tax laws; brochure advises consulting a tax advisor.
Available Riders: LIC’s Accidental Death and Disability Benefit Rider; LIC’s Accident Benefit Rider; LIC’s New Term Assurance Rider; LIC’s Premium Waiver Benefit Rider (with detailed eligibility rules, e.g., Premium Waiver on proposer’s life for minor policies).
Key Advantages: Guaranteed, fixed benefits (no dependence on future bonus performance); choice between Regular and Flexi income options; income for life after a limited PPT; whole-life death cover with guaranteed additions; high Sum Assured rebate; loan facility for liquidity; option to take death benefit in instalments.
Limitations or Gotchas: No maturity lump-sum; benefits are mainly income plus death cover. Minimum Sum Assured of ₹5 lakh is relatively high. Income starts only from a later policy year (11th–19th depending on PPT), so there is a waiting period for income. Being non-par, there is no upside from bonuses. Surrender values and loan limits are formula-based and can be relatively low in early years.
